data.body:
# Gate correction + CI second-vote follow-on (post Opus critical-review)

## Corrections (SUPERSEDE earlier plan-of-record notes in this log)
- The v1 code gate is a SINGLE deterministic vote: the Gerrit `LLM-Review` label, cast by the deterministic pass-3 blocker on thresholds. NOT the earlier-noted three-label `Code-Review=MAX AND LLM-Review=MAX AND Verified=MAX` requirement. There is exactly ONE code-review vote in v1 — no separate human Code-Review and no CI Verified vote yet. (E1 d251 AC corrected.)
- completion_verifier is OUT OF SCOPE for the reviewer-migration epic (9da1): NOT migrated, unchanged. (E2 AC corrected.)
- The Opus critical review's endorsed must-fixes were REJECTED; the design stands. The single-vote clarification + completion_verifier-out-of-scope dissolve that review's "bots-approving-bots / who-casts-Code-Review" and "completion_verifier doesn't fit the three-pass" findings.

## Intended end state: TWO-vote gate (LLM review AND CI)
- The intended code gate requires BOTH LLM review AND CI checks (build / test / lint / format) to pass — two INDEPENDENT Gerrit votes: `LLM-Review` (v1, deterministic pass-3) + `Verified` (CI). CI integration needs more design work, so it is deferred to follow-on epic 1fa8-9db4-7c1c-48f3 ("Add CI checks as a second gate vote (Verified)"), which updates the submit requirement to `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND label:Verified=MAX AND -has:unresolved`. E4 depends_on E1 (d251) and relates_to E3 (b744).
